# Questions in Livestream 117

Live Stream #117: Celebrating 10,000 Subscribers

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=834" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">13:54</a> | What does Julian Cooper's work imply for locating Sinai and evaluating Jebel Musa? | He says Cooper's place-name work deserves serious attention and that Jebel Musa remains far more plausible than fringe alternatives like Jebel al-Lawz. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=1101" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">18:21</a> | Were papyri and parchment expensive around the time of Jesus? | He says yes, though many people still saved to buy biblical text copies, and the wider Roman world was more literate than often assumed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=1308" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">21:48</a> | What evidence supports his theory of broader ancient literacy? | He points to literacy among miners, craftsmen, draftsmen, scribes, military men, rulers, and slaves, while noting variation by period. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=1696" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">28:16</a> | What would Israelites or Egyptians have called Egypt itself? | He distinguishes the terms used for the two lands, Semitic Mizraim, and later labels like Kemet, stressing that modern uses can flatten those distinctions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=2029" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">33:49</a> | How should people be weaned off Ivan Panin and Bible-code style numerology? | He calls the method eisegetical and gnostic in tendency, arguing that pattern-hunting is not how the biblical authors wrote. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=2397" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">39:57</a> | Why is Bible-code numerology eisegetical? | He says it finds coincidences unrelated to the text's message or authorial intent and treats those patterns as hidden meaning. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=2640" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">44:00</a> | What do the Kadesh inscriptions tell us about Egyptian military tents and how can we tell what really happened in the battle? | He points to surviving material parallels for tents and says the battle accounts must be read with common sense against their royal hyperbole. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=2924" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">48:44</a> | Why are Jephthah and Samson praised in Hebrews 11 if they were such flawed judges? | He says Hebrews is commending particular acts of faith, not giving blanket moral approval to their lives. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=3551" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">59:11</a> | Is he an expert in the Stone, Silver, and Iron Ages? | He says his expertise is primarily the Bronze Age, though he knows adjacent Stone and Iron Age material where it overlaps. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=3783" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:03:03</a> | Why does he not move to a cheaper country or city? | He says moving would require money, reliable internet, suitable work or immigration arrangements, and enough resources to restart safely. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=3983" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:06:23</a> | Why does he not organize tours of Egypt? | He says they tried when the channel was smaller, but tours or courses need a critical mass of interested people and practical support. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=4135" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:08:55</a> | Why are the pyramids a problem for young-earth creationist flood chronologies? | He says some chronologies put the flood during Khufu's pyramid construction, which creates a major conflict with Egyptian chronology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=4672" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:17:52</a> | In Isaiah 7:14, should the phrase be read as "has conceived" or "shall conceive"? | He works through the Hebrew and says the surrounding future language makes the sign future-oriented. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=5226" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:27:06</a> | What does unleavened bread represent in Passover from a historical point of view? | He says it represents haste: Israel had to be ready to leave before there was time for ordinary leavening. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=5873" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:37:53</a> | How is Genesis 6 not referring to all women if it says mankind multiplied and daughters were born to them? | He argues the wording and "sons of God" language are cultic, not a generic reference to all men and women. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=6558" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:49:18</a> | What are his thoughts on UFO disclosures, and what if aliens claimed they built the pyramids? | He says he is an evidentialist: there is a real UFO phenomenon, but claims about pyramids still need evidence and would not override known worker records. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=7052" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:57:32</a> | Why are demigods or mortal children of deities not present in Egyptian mythology like in other ancient religions? | He says Egyptian royal divine sonship works differently from Greek-style demigods, and the reason for the difference is not fully clear. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=7424" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:03:44</a> | Are there good books that counter Mormonism? | He recommends an older edition of Walter Martin's Kingdom of the Cults chapter on Mormonism, while noting Martin's polemical style. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=7530" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:05:30</a> | Has he seen Sound of Freedom? | He says he has not seen it yet because he has been busy, but he has heard good things and plans to watch it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=7630" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:07:10</a> | Does Bithiah, Pharaoh's daughter in 1 Chronicles 4:17, have anything to do with Moses? | He discusses the text as a possible Egyptian royal connection but treats the identification cautiously. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=7912" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:11:52</a> | Why did Satan urge Judas to kill Jesus if Jesus' death undermined Satan's goals? | He says Satan is not omniscient and did not fully understand the prophecies or the consequences of Christ's death. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=8255" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:17:35</a> | Does Genesis 47:11 prove that the land of Rameses need not refer to Pi-Ramesses but to a later renamed region? | He says Genesis includes retrojection, so the reference need not prove the place already had that name in Joseph's time. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=8828" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:27:08</a> | Are there recent scholarly works on gematria in ancient Greece, the ancient Near East, and the Bible? | He recommends Behind the Scenes of the Old Testament for biblical ancient Near Eastern background and says gematria may require article-level research. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=8909" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:28:29</a> | If he wrote a book on improving Sunday school, what would the table of contents be? | He would focus on essentials of the faith, increasing complexity through repetition, and how to teach rather than prescribing one exact curriculum. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=9800" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:43:20</a> | What underappreciated lessons does the Old Testament have for missiology? | He says Israel was supposed to be a kingdom of priests for the nations, and the Old Testament often shows righteous gentiles exposing Israel's failures. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=10032" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:47:12</a> | What is the connection between the Great Hymn to the Aten and Psalm 104? | He sees real literary borrowing or intertextuality, with Psalm 104 redeeming similar language for Yahweh worship rather than Aten worship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=10314" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:51:54</a> | What are his thoughts on Oriental Orthodoxy? | He sees it as a bona fide branch of Christianity, while noting concerns about expanded canon and some practices. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=10566" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:56:06</a> | What is the concern with the expanded canon? | He says many apocryphal books contain serious historical or theological errors, though they remain useful for cultural context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=11455" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:10:55</a> | What is his view of Liberty University? | He says it is accredited and can provide a quality education, but Old Testament study there will come through a fundamentalist lens. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=11577" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:12:57</a> | Is Satan omnipresent? | He says no; Satan and demons are limited to one place at a time, though they can coordinate in groups. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=11687" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:14:47</a> | Is there evidence for the twenty percent tax associated with Joseph in Egypt? | He says Egyptian evidence for large royal extraction and taxation makes the biblical picture intelligible rather than outlandish. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=11838" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:17:18</a> | If God can appear anywhere, what is the purpose of building sacred spaces like the temple? | He says sacred space provides a proper context for sinful people to approach and relate to a holy God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=12115" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:21:55</a> | What grammars does he recommend for autodidacts learning Biblical Hebrew, Aramaic, and Greek? | He recommends standard classroom grammars, including Mounce for Greek and Kelley for Hebrew, with answer keys and audio helps where available. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=12342" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:25:42</a> | What should be made of the silos built at Tell el-Daba and Tell el-Retaba during the early 18th Dynasty? | He says they do not prove too much by themselves, since the sites have mixed 18th- and 19th-Dynasty evidence and later disturbance. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=12493" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:28:13</a> | What is his view of tithing requirements in churches today? | He supports tithing as worship but opposes enforced church requirements, saying the amount should be a matter of conscience in modern contexts. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=12841" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:34:01</a> | What would be a reasonable age of the earth for a creationist? | He treats that as a separate creationist debate but says only a very compressed young-earth view forces the issue sharply. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=12968" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:36:08</a> | Should communion use wine or juice? | He says wine is the historical norm, but the practice depends on denomination and pastoral context, including alcohol concerns or unsafe local water. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=13335" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:42:15</a> | Is there a Christian exemption to serving wine to children? | He says it depends on tradition; Eastern Orthodoxy communes children with wine, and Scripture does not give a simple universal age rule. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=13594" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:46:34</a> | How intensive was trade between Egypt and Nubia or other southern peoples, and what was traded? | He says Nubia under Egyptian control was more tribute than trade, while Punt involved exchange of Egyptian bronze tools for gold and malachite ore. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=13736" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:48:56</a> | Has he tried sake, and what are his thoughts? | He says he likes sake and has made rice wine, though he is not drinking it while on keto. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/mW67gRhiXgs?t=14122" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:55:22</a> | How could Sadducees deny angels when angels appear in the Torah? | He says their materialist, Torah-only reading strategy pushed them to allegorize or minimize those scenes. |
